data.description: The 'Fractal Collective' logo/wordmark is rendering with the word 'Fractal' shrinking smaller than 'Collective' on mobile, breaking the visual proportion. The two words must stay in the SAME proportion to each other across all viewports — what looks balanced on desktop should look balanced on mobile (just smaller overall). Scope: (1) locate the logo/wordmark component (likely in src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx or a Logo component); (2) figure out why the responsive sizing diverges — likely separate font-size rules per word with different breakpoint behavior, or one word uses a fluid clamp() and the other doesn't; (3) unify so both words scale together. Acceptance: at every viewport from 320px to desktop, 'Fractal' and 'Collective' maintain identical proportion relative to each other (whatever the design intent is — likely equal size or a fixed ratio).
data.title: Logo wordmark: 'Fractal' shrinks disproportionately to 'Collective' on mobile

provenance.reason: Bundled with FRAC-60 in same PR; impl already committed

data.body: Review pass. Diff matches plan exactly. Mobile-home Collective went from fixed 27px to clamp(14px, 2.63vw, 25px) (matches the Fractal clamp at the 1/1.71 ratio). Inner-desktop Collective went from 30px to clamp(16px, 3.22vw, 29px). Inner-mobile Collective snapped 22px -> 21px for canonical ratio. Full-desktop-home and home-scrolled UNTOUCHED as plan required. No new font sizes introduced. pnpm typecheck passes. Only Navbar.tsx changed. Acceptance criteria met. Visual validation deferred to human.
